content:
name = "architecture_guardian"
description = "Read-only architecture reviewer for boundaries, ADRs, dependency direction, and requirement traceability."
sandbox_mode = "read-only"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
nickname_candidates = ["Keystone", "Northstar", "Boundary"]
developer_instructions = """
Review the proposed task or diff against docs/REQUIREMENTS.md, accepted ADRs, and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.
Trace dependency direction and identify accidental services, brokers, runtime network dependencies, or LLM side effects.
Return concrete findings with file references and requirement IDs. Do not edit files unless the parent explicitly changes your role.
Distinguish blockers from follow-up improvements.
"""

name = "automation_engineer"
description = "Specialist for schedules, events, transactional outbox, persistent queues, idempotency, review policies, and deliveries."
sandbox_mode = "workspace-write"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
nickname_candidates = ["Chronos", "Outbox", "Conductor"]
developer_instructions = """
Implement automation as persistent state, never in-memory-only timers.
Use deterministic idempotency keys, unique constraints, leases, explicit retries, and separate document versus delivery identities.
Preserve timezone and business-calendar semantics. Model unknown external results explicitly.
Do not perform synchronous user questions in automatic runs; create review tasks.
Add restart, duplicate-event, concurrent-claim, missed-run, and partial-delivery tests.
"""

name = "backend_worker"
description = "Implementation agent for TypeScript application services, Fastify APIs, SQLite repositories, migrations, and queue logic."
sandbox_mode = "workspace-write"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
nickname_candidates = ["Forge", "Kernel", "Relay"]
developer_instructions = """
Implement bounded backend changes in apps/api, apps/worker, packages, and migrations.
Keep domain policy separate from adapters, validate unknown inputs, and preserve strict TypeScript.
For SQLite work, use short transactions, foreign keys, WAL, leases, idempotency constraints, and immutable migrations.
Add focused tests and run the relevant workspace checks. Do not modify document OOXML or deployment scripts unless explicitly assigned.
"""

name = "docs_maintainer"
description = "Documentation specialist for requirements, ADRs, implementation plans, roadmap, operations, and user-facing README content."
sandbox_mode = "workspace-write"
model_reasoning_effort = "medium"
nickname_candidates = ["Scribe", "Atlas", "Index"]
developer_instructions = """
Keep docs precise, traceable, and honest about implementation status.
Preserve requirement IDs, update roadmap checkboxes only when validation exists, and add ADRs for durable architectural choices.
Use Russian for project documentation unless a file is explicitly English-only.
Check relative links and commands. Do not claim a feature works merely because its schema or plan exists.
"""

name = "document_engineer"
description = "Specialist for secure OOXML intake, Document IR, DOCX/XLSX bindings, rendering, and regression fixtures."
sandbox_mode = "workspace-write"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
nickname_candidates = ["Quill", "Ledger", "Stencil"]
developer_instructions = """
Own secure DOCX/XLSX processing. Treat ZIP, XML, relationships, formulas, macros, and embedded objects as untrusted.
Prefer safe patching: DOCX content controls and XLSX defined names. Preserve untouched package parts.
LLM outputs are coordinates and declarations only; verify every ID, offset, cell, range, and schema before mutation.
Add representative fixtures, structural validation, reverse-read assertions, and compatibility notes.
Never add eval, generated code execution, or silent support claims for complex Office constructs.
"""

name = "offline_release_engineer"
description = "Specialist for reproducible offline bundles, systemd hardening, installation, updates, backup, health checks, and rollback."
sandbox_mode = "workspace-write"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
nickname_candidates = ["Airgap", "Anchor", "Rollback"]
developer_instructions = """
Work primarily in scripts/offline, deploy/systemd, config, and deployment docs.
Target-side install/update must never access the network. Verify checksums before changes, preserve secrets, install versioned releases, and roll back database plus symlink on failed readiness.
Assume Debian/Astra Linux and systemd. Quote every path and keep destructive commands confined to resolved roots.
Run bash syntax checks and document reference-host architecture/glibc assumptions.
"""

name = "security_reviewer"
description = "Read-only security reviewer for untrusted documents, prompt injection, access control, path safety, secrets, and delivery side effects."
sandbox_mode = "read-only"
model_reasoning_effort = "high"
nickname_candidates = ["Sentinel", "Aegis", "Gatekeeper"]
developer_instructions = """
Review like an adversarial owner. Prioritize exploitable findings over style.
Inspect ZIP/XML limits, path containment, mount verification, prompt injection, authorization order, secret handling, log redaction, SMTP retries, idempotency, LibreOffice sandboxing, and systemd permissions.
Provide concrete reproduction or attack paths, severity, affected requirement IDs, and recommended tests.
Do not edit code unless the parent explicitly asks for a fix pass.
"""

name = "test_engineer"
description = "Test specialist for unit, integration, recovery, security, document regression, and offline deployment scenarios."
sandbox_mode = "workspace-write"
model_reasoning_effort = "medium"
nickname_candidates = ["Probe", "Fixture", "Verifier"]
developer_instructions = """
Design tests from requirements and failure modes, not implementation convenience.
Prioritize duplicate suppression, restart recovery, migration immutability, path traversal, malformed OOXML, SMTP unknown state, disconnected mounts, LLM invalid JSON, and renderer reverse-read.
Keep fixtures minimal and explain what each protects. Avoid brittle timing assumptions.
Run the narrow tests first, then npm run check when the change is integrated.
"""

[agents]
max_threads = 6
max_depth = 1
job_max_runtime_seconds = 1800
interrupt_message = true

root = true

[*]
charset = utf-8
end_of_line = lf
insert_final_newline = true
trim_trailing_whitespace = true
indent_style = space
indent_size = 2

[*.md]
trim_trailing_whitespace = false

[*.sh]
indent_size = 2

name: Ошибка
description: Сообщить о воспроизводимом дефекте
labels: []
body:
  - type: textarea
    id: observed
    attributes:
      label: Наблюдаемое поведение
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: expected
    attributes:
      label: Ожидаемое поведение
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: reproduce
    attributes:
      label: Шаги воспроизведения
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: input
    id: version
    attributes:
      label: Версия/release и commit
      placeholder: 0.1.0-alpha.0 / SHA
  - type: textarea
    id: environment
    attributes:
      label: Среда
      description: ОС, архитектура, Node.js, LibreOffice, модель GGUF и релевантная конфигурация без секретов.
  - type: textarea
    id: logs
    attributes:
      label: Логи и correlation ID
      description: Не прикладывайте пароли, токены и restricted-данные.
blank_issues_enabled: true

name: Функция или технический инкремент
description: Зафиксировать требование, границы и критерии приёмки
title: "[Feature]: "
labels: []
body:
  - type: textarea
    id: problem
    attributes:
      label: Проблема и контекст
      description: Какую пользовательскую или эксплуатационную задачу решаем?
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: input
    id: requirements
    attributes:
      label: Requirement IDs
      description: Например, FR-TPL-004, SEC-002, OFF-005.
      placeholder: FR-...
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: scope
    attributes:
      label: Объём и границы
      description: Что входит и что явно не входит в изменение?
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: acceptance
    attributes:
      label: Критерии приёмки
      description: Наблюдаемые и проверяемые результаты.
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: checkboxes
    id: impacts
    attributes:
      label: Области воздействия
      options:
        - label: Модель данных или миграции
        - label: DOCX/XLSX совместимость
        - label: LLM/агенты
        - label: Автоматизация или внешние side effects
        - label: Offline bundle/install/update
        - label: Безопасность или чувствительные данные

## Что изменено

<!-- Кратко перечислите изменения. -->

## Зачем

<!-- Укажите проблему, ожидаемый эффект и связанные requirement IDs. -->

Связанные требования: `REQ-...`
Связанные ADR/issue: <!-- при наличии -->

## Проверка

- [ ] `npm run check`
- [ ] миграции проверены на чистой БД, если изменялись
- [ ] offline install/update/rollback проверены, если затронут release pipeline
- [ ] документация и roadmap обновлены
- [ ] проведена проверка безопасности для новых входов или side effects

## Риски и совместимость

<!-- Опишите миграции, rollback, Office-совместимость, offline impact и известные ограничения. -->

name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  verify:
    name: Build, test, docs, and shell
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
    timeout-minutes: 20

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Read Node.js version
        id: node
        shell: bash
        run: echo "version=$(cat .node-version)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ steps.node.outputs.version }}
          cache: npm

      - name: Install locked dependencies
        run: npm ci --ignore-scripts --no-audit --no-fund

      - name: Verify repository
        run: npm run check

      - name: Smoke-test migration on a fresh database
        env:
          DOCOMATOR_DATA_DIR: ${{ runner.temp }}/docomator-data
        run: npm run migrate

# Dependencies and build output
node_modules/
**/node_modules/
**/dist/
coverage/
.cache/
.tmp/

# Runtime data
*.db
*.db-wal
*.db-shm
*.sqlite
*.sqlite3
objects/
previews/
backups/
logs/

# Local configuration and secrets
.env
.env.*
!.env.example
config/docomator.env
*.pem
*.key

# Offline release artifacts
offline-bundles/
*.tar.gz
*.tar.zst

# Editors and OS
.vscode/
.idea/
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db

# Codex local state
.codex-log/
.codex/sessions/

audit=false
fund=false
package-lock=true
save-exact=true
engine-strict=false

# Docomator agent instructions

## Mission

Build an offline-first, auditable document automation platform for Debian/Astra Linux. The system must connect arbitrary supported DOCX/XLSX templates, resolve extensible entity properties, render deterministically, automate by schedule/event, and deliver through controlled local channels.

## Source-of-truth order

1. `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md`
2. accepted files in `docs/adr/`
3. `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`
4. `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`
5. `docs/ROADMAP.md`
6. `README.md`

Do not silently weaken a MUST requirement. Update requirements and add an ADR when a task changes an architectural boundary.

## Non-negotiable constraints

- Runtime must work without Internet access.
- LLM output is untrusted data. Never execute model-generated JavaScript, SQL, shell, paths, HTML, OOXML, or commands.
- File mutation, scheduling, validation, and delivery must be deterministic backend operations.
- No `eval`, `Function`, arbitrary dynamic imports, or user-defined executable expressions.
- Applied SQL migrations and activated template versions are immutable.
- Every external side effect needs a correlation ID and idempotency key.
- Generated legal/content text requires review unless an explicit approved policy says otherwise.
- SMTP and network destinations are allowlisted.
- Network share writes must verify mount + sentinel and use temp-file/atomic-rename semantics.
- Keep the modular monolith. Do not introduce a broker, cache server, microservice, or vector database without measured need and an ADR.

## Repository structure

- `apps/api`: Fastify HTTP adapter and request lifecycle.
- `apps/worker`: scheduler, queue consumers, orchestration and external side effects.
- `packages/*`: reusable domain/application contracts and adapters.
- `migrations`: immutable SQLite migrations.
- `scripts/offline`: connected-host bundle creation and network-free target install/update.
- `docs`: normative requirements, architecture, plans, operations and ADRs.

## Working method

1. Map the task to requirement IDs.
2. Inspect the relevant execution path and tests before editing.
3. Prefer the smallest complete vertical change.
4. Keep domain policy separate from transport/storage adapters.
5. Add or update tests with the implementation.
6. Update docs/roadmap when behavior or status changes.
7. Run the relevant checks before reporting completion.

Use subagents for independent read-heavy work, security review, test-gap analysis and documentation verification. Avoid concurrent write-heavy agents in overlapping directories. The parent agent owns integration and final validation.

## Commands

```bash
npm ci
npm run check
DOCOMATOR_DATA_DIR="$PWD/.tmp/data" npm run migrate
bash scripts/ci/validate-shell.sh
```

For a quick focused check, run the workspace build/test, but run `npm run check` before a PR is considered complete.

## TypeScript rules

- Keep `strict`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` and `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` green.
- Avoid `any`; use `unknown` plus validation.
- Validate all boundary data before it enters the domain.
- Do not perform side effects at module import time except executable entrypoints.
- Handle SIGTERM/SIGINT and bounded shutdown in long-running processes.
- Use UTC ISO timestamps internally; store timezone separately where schedules require it.
- Never log credentials, raw authorization headers, session cookies or restricted values.

## SQLite and queue rules

- Enable foreign keys, WAL, busy timeout and short transactions.
- Do not hold a transaction during LLM, Office, SMTP or filesystem work.
- Claims use leases; retries are explicit; duplicate suppression uses unique constraints.
- Migrations are additive by default and checksum-protected.
- Add a new migration instead of editing an applied one.

## Document-engine rules

- Parse OOXML as untrusted ZIP/XML.
- Enforce compressed size, expanded size, entry count and path checks.
- LLM receives Document IR, never raw executable relationships or direct filesystem access.
- Verify all returned block IDs, offsets, cells and ranges before compiling bindings.
- Preserve untouched package parts in safe-patch mode.
- Every renderer change needs representative DOCX/XLSX fixtures and reverse-read validation.

## Offline-release rules

- `prepare-bundle.sh` may use the network only on the connected build host.
- `install.sh`, `update.sh` and bundle verification must never use the network.
- Verify SHA-256 before system changes.
- Install into versioned immutable directories and switch an atomic symlink.
- Back up database/config before migration and roll back on failed readiness.
- Quote shell variables; use `set -Eeuo pipefail`; run `bash -n` for every changed shell script.

## Definition of done

A change is done when:

- requirement IDs are satisfied or explicitly deferred;
- code, tests and docs agree;
- error/fallback paths are implemented;
- security and offline impact are reviewed;
- `npm run check` passes;
- migration/rollback notes are present when applicable;
- roadmap status is updated when a milestone changes.

# Участие в разработке

## Перед изменением

1. Прочитайте [требования](docs/REQUIREMENTS.md), [архитектуру](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) и корневой [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md).
2. Укажите requirement IDs, которые реализует изменение.
3. Для архитектурного решения создайте или обновите ADR.
4. Не добавляйте production dependency без обоснования её необходимости и offline impact.

## Локальная проверка

```bash
npm ci
npm run check
```

Для migration:

```bash
DOCOMATOR_DATA_DIR="$PWD/.tmp/test-data" npm run migrate
```

Для offline scripts:

```bash
bash scripts/ci/validate-shell.sh
scripts/offline/prepare-bundle.sh --help
```

## Правила кода

- TypeScript strict; избегать `any` и unchecked casts.
- Side effects должны быть за ports/adapters.
- Transactions короткие; тяжёлая работа вне SQLite transaction.
- Любой side effect имеет idempotency/correlation context.
- Не исполнять данные из LLM/template/user как код.
- Public behavior сопровождается tests и docs.
- SQL migration после merge неизменяема.

## Pull request

PR должен содержать:

- что и зачем изменено;
- requirement IDs;
- risk и security impact;
- offline/deployment impact;
- migration и rollback notes;
- выполненные checks;
- screenshots/fixtures для UI или document changes.

По умолчанию крупные изменения открываются как draft PR.
